title: Work-item-aware code chat: functional behavior Q&A linked to work-item history

steps.description: Pipeline: extend orch/rag/job.py + orch/rag/indexer.py to populate LanceDB docs_{project_id} table with design-doc embeddings for every work item with design_doc_content; keyed by (project_id, work_item_id) with type/title/summary/dates metadata; full + incremental modes
Backend: phase-aware QAEngine state machine; hybrid retrieval (code LanceDB + docs LanceDB + Postgres FTS); git-log file→work-item resolver; LLM query classifier; citation allowlist enforcement

API: extend /api/projects/{id}/code/qa SSE with phase event type; extend _CitationTracker and citation event payload with work_item_type/work_item_id; consolidate /findusages routing into the new pipeline

Frontend: stream.js phase handler; render.js work-item chip variant; new chat/parts work_item_chip.html, work_item_feed.html, phase_strip.html; composer.js /why /history slash aliases + tone-switch chip; citation popover links to item detail page

Tests: unit tests (phase events, classifier, citation allowlist, git-log resolver, hybrid retrieval merge); integration tests (full SSE flow, slash override, classifier auto-detect, findusages, no regression); eval set of ≥10 (question, expected-shape, must-cite) tuples against current iw-ai-core baseline
Code review: S09 (test coverage vs AC)
Final cross-agent review: pipeline ↔ backend ↔ api ↔ frontend ↔ tests integration, AC coverage, no regressions to default code-only chat
Fix CRITICAL and HIGH findings from S11
QV: Browser verification — /why slash command produces phase events, work-item feed, and citation chips linking to item detail pages; default code question has no regressions
